name: forge-indian-national-parks-catalog
description: Canonical 25-park catalog for Forge's Indian folk-art wildlife pipeline. Each park anchors 4 species (2 mammals + 2 birds) chosen for park-iconic representation. Geographic balance: 4 Himalayan/North, 6 East/Northeast, 4 Central, 6 West/Desert, 5 Western Ghats/South. Bird-sanctuary parks (Keoladeo, Chilika, Velavadar) over-rotate on birds; marine parks (Marine Kutch, Bhitarkanika) over-rotate on aquatic/coastal species — these breakers from the strict 2+2 rule are intentional and documented per-park.

jim-corbett.significance: India's oldest national park; launchpad of Project Tiger (1973). Named for hunter-turned-conservationist Jim Corbett.

kaziranga.significance: UNESCO World Heritage Site. Holds two-thirds of the world's one-horned rhinoceros population.

ranthambore.significance: Former hunting ground of Maharajas of Jaipur; among India's best parks for daytime tiger sightings. Houses the 10th-century Ranthambore Fort.

bandhavgarh.significance: Highest tiger density of any Indian park. The Bandhavgarh fort sits on a 2,000-year-old rock outcrop within the park.

kanha.significance: Inspiration for Kipling's Jungle Book. Sole habitat of the hard-ground barasingha (swamp deer) — saved from extinction here.

pench.significance: Straddles MP and Maharashtra. The Pench river flows through the park. Kipling's Mowgli setting in part.

tadoba-andhari.significance: Maharashtra's oldest and largest national park. Tadoba lake is the centerpiece — famed for marsh-crocodile and tiger overlap.

sundarbans.significance: UNESCO World Heritage Site. The world's largest mangrove forest. Home to mangrove-adapted Bengal tigers that swim daily.

gir.significance: The world's only home of the Asiatic lion outside captivity. Population recovered from 20 (1913) to over 700 today.

periyar.significance: Artificial Periyar Lake at the park's heart — boat safaris are the iconic experience. UNESCO Western Ghats inscription.

bandipur.significance: Part of the Nilgiri Biosphere Reserve. Forms a contiguous protected landscape with Nagarhole, Mudumalai, and Wayanad.

nagarhole.significance: Highest density of herbivores in Asia. Named for the snake-shaped (nagara-hole) Kabini river that flows through it.

silent-valley.significance: Saved by the Save Silent Valley movement (1973-1985) — one of the world's most successful grassroots ecological campaigns. Cicada-free silence gives the park its name.

anamalai.significance: Anamalai means 'elephant mountain' in Tamil. Endemic Lion-Tailed Macaque stronghold; shola-grassland ecosystem.

hemis.significance: India's largest national park; highest density of snow leopards in the world. Home to Hemis monastery, Ladakh's largest.

great-himalayan.significance: UNESCO World Heritage Site (2014). Glacial-fed Sainj, Tirthan, and Jiwa Nal valleys converge here. Last refuge of the Western Tragopan.

khangchendzonga.significance: UNESCO mixed Natural-Cultural World Heritage Site (2016). Centered on Khangchendzonga peak (world's 3rd highest). Sacred to Sikkimese Buddhist and Lepcha traditions.

namdapha.significance: Only park in the world where four big cats coexist (tiger, leopard, snow leopard, clouded leopard). Massive elevation range across one contiguous park.

manas.significance: UNESCO World Heritage Site. Project Tiger, Project Elephant, AND Biosphere Reserve. Contiguous with Royal Manas in Bhutan.

bhitarkanika.significance: Largest saltwater crocodile habitat in India. Mangrove tidal forests at the Brahmani-Baitarani delta. Olive Ridley turtle nesting at Gahirmatha beach (adjacent).

chilika.significance: Asia's largest brackish-water lagoon. Ramsar wetland. Winter destination for ~1 million migratory birds. Endemic Irrawaddy dolphin population.
chilika._breaks_2_2_rule: lagoon park — mammal options limited; one mammal (dolphin) + one terrestrial-island mammal

keoladeo.significance: UNESCO World Heritage Site. Former duck-shooting ground of Bharatpur maharajas. Once-historical Siberian Crane wintering ground.

sariska.significance: Aravalli range setting. First park to successfully reintroduce tigers after local extinction (2008). Ancient Kankwari fort within the park.

marine-kutch.display_name: Marine National Park, Gulf of Kutch
marine-kutch.significance: India's first Marine National Park. 42 islands, coral reefs, dugong habitat. Whale shark aggregation site along the adjacent Saurashtra coast.
marine-kutch._breaks_2_2_rule: marine park — 'mammals' include cetaceans + sirenian; birds are coastal/shore

velavadar.significance: World's largest blackbuck congregation. Largest roost of Harriers (Montagu's, Pallid, Marsh) in the subcontinent — wintering 2000+ birds.

_notes._2_2_rule_breakers: chilika, marine-kutch (marine/lagoon — limited mammal options), keoladeo (bird sanctuary — over-rotates on birds historically but balanced for catalog purposes)
_notes.iconic_species_naming: species slugs ending in '-<park>' (e.g., 'saltwater-crocodile-bhitarkanika', 'red-panda-khangchendzonga') indicate park-localized variants where the same species appears across multiple parks — distinguishes the Bhitarkanika saltwater croc from the Sundarbans one, even when biologically identical, for catalog uniqueness.

_changelog.1.0.0: Initial 25-park canonical catalog. 4 new parks added vs prior 21-park animals.json: bhitarkanika, great-himalayan, khangchendzonga, nagarhole. Region-balanced (5 Himalayan, 5 NE, 4 Central, 6 W/Desert, 5 Western Ghats/S). Per-park metadata includes biome, elevation, area, established year, conservation significance, and iconic species roster.
